data.title: Brookfield Business Partners had a successful year end in 2025, generating over $2 billion in capital, investing $700 million in acquisitions, and repurchasing $235 million of units. Adjusted EBITDA was $2,409 million, and net income for the year was $43 million. The company is nearing the completion of a corporate reorganization to enhance global demand for its shares. The strategic acquisition of Fosber is expected to enhance growth and efficiency. Corporate liquidity is approximately $2,600 million. Repurchases of 8.8 million units and shares were made. The company plans to declare a quarterly dividend of $0.0625 per share.
data.description: Brookfield Business Partners announced strong financial results for the year ended December 31, 2025. They generated over $2 billion from capital recycling, invested $700 million in acquisitions, and repurchased $235 million of shares. Net income for 2025 was $43 million compared to a loss of $109 million in 2024. Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was $2.41 billion. By segment, Industrials saw a 10% increase in EBITDA, Business Services remained stable, and Infrastructure Services decreased due to divestitures. They also announced acquiring Fosber for $480 million and corporate reorganization plans. They repurchased $72 million worth of shares and ended the year with $2.135 billion in liquidity. They plan to declare a quarterly dividend of $0.0625 per share. For more details, visit their website. They had $75.761 billion in assets and $75.474 billion in liabilities and equity as of December 31, 2025.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin prices drop 7%, leading to a surge in liquidations to $1.8 billion. Bitcoin hits $82,000 with high ETF outflows ($817.9M for Bitcoin and $155.6M for Ethereum). Meme coin market cap drops to $40.6B amid broader selloff.
data.description: Bitcoin soared to $82,000 in a single night, triggering $1.80 billion in liquidations in the last 24 hours. Bitcoin ETFs experienced $817.9 million in net outflows, while Ethereum ETFs saw $155.6 million in net outflows yesterday. The meme coin sector plummeted by 9% over the past day, resulting in a total market capitalization of $40.6 billion amid a widespread selloff. Current cryptocurrency prices are as follows: Bitcoin at $82,657.91, Ethereum at $2,731.64, Solana at $115.78, XRP at $1.74, Dogecoin at $0.1138, and Shiba Inu at $0.057219. Analyst insights and further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Copper hits all-time high at $14,268/ton. $200 billion merger may face challenges despite market rally and global demand.
data.description: On Thursday, the market experienced its most extensive selloff of 2026, with copper standing out by reaching a record high of $14,268 per metric ton on the London Metal Exchange. This surge comes amidst a challenging environment as the mining industry is contemplating a significant $200 billion merger between Rio Tinto Plc and Glencore Plc. Factors driving the rally include a weakening U.S. dollar, speculative momentum, and the narrative surrounding copper's pivotal role in the technology transition. This surge is further supported by the demand from sectors such as electrification, AI, defense, and data centers that heavily rely on copper. This unprecedented rise in copper price signifies a departure from being a mere indicator of economic health, as it is now seen as more complex and intertwined with various sectors.
